# Contributing Guide

## Opening an Issue

### Before you post
- Check if your bug or idea has already been reported.
- Choose `Bug Report` or `Suggestion/Enhancement` when creating an issue. Issues that ignore the template may be closed without response.

### Writing a good bug report
- Describe what happens and what should happen instead.
- Include steps to reproduce the problem.
  - Always include the versions of the mods you are using. "Latest" is not a version.
- Attach screenshots or crash logs if relevant.

### Writing a good suggestion
- Explain what you want and why it would be useful.
- If it's a niche personal preference rather than a general improvement, it may not be accepted.

### What will get your issue closed immediately
- "It doesn't work" with no other details.
- Duplicate of an existing open issue.
- Asking for support for unofficial forks or modpacks.

---

## Pull Requests

1. Open an issue first for anything significant. Don't spend time on a big PR that might not be accepted.
2. Keep changes focused, one fix or feature per PR.
3. Test your changes before submitting.
4. Describe what your PR does and why in the description.

---

## Code Style
- Follow the existing formatting conventions in the codebase.
- Comment anything that isn't immediately obvious.

---

If you're unsure about anything, ask in the issue before starting.
